programme.desc: Tony and the team try to prove Ziva had nothing to do with a political assassination carried out by her former Mossad partner - but have to tackle the case without Gibbs, who has retired to Mexico and now spends his time building boats. Naval crime drama, starring Cote de Pablo and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Against his better judgement, Gibbs comes out of retirement to help Fornell and his daughter, whose lives are being threatened by an escaped convict. The team reopens the criminal's case and questions a former sailor, the only person to have contacted him while he was in jail. Starring Mark Harmon, David McCallum, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette, with Hal Holbrook

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A missing US Navy lieutenant's car is found covered in blood, and the team discovers she had been profiling men to find the perfect partner. When further investigations reveal she had planned to attend a seminar at a local hotel, Ziva goes undercover in a bid to find out more. Meanwhile, Jenny makes Tony a secret offer

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The shooting of a petty officer looks like an open-and-shut case when a Russian man is found with a recently fired gun in his car. However, the investigation takes a dramatic turn when it is revealed he is working as a spy for the Department of Homeland Security. Military crime drama, with Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a lance corporal, whose body has been found in an empty house, and discovers he had a number of spurned lovers and a $2million inheritance. Ducky and Gibbs interview one of the dead marine's partners, but disagree over whether she is telling the truth. David McCallum and Mark Harmon star

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: As they await the verdict of Cabe's trial, the team tries to focus their energy on rescuing a diabetic dog trapped underground

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: On Christmas Eve, after Walter chooses work over spending time with his friends, he hits his head and enters a dream world where Team Scorpion never existed

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: New Mossad director Orli Elbaz asks for the team's help in finding Ilan Bodnar when one of her agents is shot dead in Virginia, telling Gibbs he was investigating several operatives who had gone rogue after Eli David's death. Ziva travels with Tony to Berlin in her pursuit of Bodnar, the man she believes was responsible for the death of her father. Guest starring Marina Sirtis (Star Trek: The Next Generation)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team tracks Bodnar's SUV to a remote house in the countryside, but inside find only his dead accomplice - identified as a South African mercenary who helped Bodnar procure diamonds. Ziva and Tony, meanwhile, discharge themselves from hospital and wheedle information out of Abby to continue investigating on the quiet, having been warned by Homeland Security to stay off the case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A cook from a US Navy destroyer tells McGee and Gibbs he believes someone is stalking him with the intention of trying to make him poison the crew, and a picture on a stolen phone leads the team to a group testing the psychological effects of surveillance. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons from the Inspector General's office closes in on Ziva as he investigates the death of Ilan Bodnar, the man who killed her father. Guest starring Colin Hanks (Gus Grimly in the Fargo TV series)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates when Navy Seal Chad McBride's wife receives a package from Riyadh in Saudi Arabia containing her husband's severed head, which Palmer discovers contains very high levels of radiation. McBride's commanding officer reveals the soldier was on a top-secret reconnaissance mission and asks Vance not to disclose the details to anyone. Meanwhile, Richard Parsons tries to get Gibbs to take responsibility for the many unnecessary deaths for which he holds the NCIS team accountable

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The American crime drama returns with the team still reeling from the Department of Defence investigation, with Ziva, McGee and Tony having handed in their badges. Parsons continues his inquiries into Gibbs and his agents, but events take a turn when an explosion kills the Secretary of the US Navy and seriously injures the director of Homeland Security. When attempts are made on the lives of Gibbs and Tony, it appears that all of the NCIS agents are in mortal danger. Starring Mark Harmon and Colin Hanks

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The investigators examine the death of US marine Tommy Hill, who appears to have been fatally stabbed en route to a homecoming party thrown in his honour by his foster family. McGee and Ziva follow a trail of blood that leads them to a mobile phone, while Abby discovers the victim had been trying to hack into Virginia's state website and Ducky's autopsy reveals that Hill had been injected with a sedative

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?

programme.title: NCIS

programme.title: Cadfael
programme.desc: The sleuthing monk defends a man accused of murdering a wealthy and powerful baron, with only a mysterious leper as his ally. Medieval drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Terence Hardiman, Michael Culver and Sarah Badel

programme.title: Cadfael

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: The discovery of a concrete-encased body at a Docklands building site leads to conflict between DCI Hunt and the location's owner. Investigations are further hampered by the disappearance of crucial evidence, an incident that points to a mole in the detective's own team. Meanwhile, Alex discovers that her fascination with the case is distracting her from returning home

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes
programme.desc: Gene learns crooked cops are going to interrupt a bullion robbery and steal the gold for themselves - a discovery that gives him the chance to root out corruption in the force once and for all. Meanwhile, the arrival of an attractive woman highlights the cracks in his relationship with Alex, who is making him doubt her trustworthiness

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The Meldrews embark on a two-week holiday in Portugal in David Renwick's feature-length special from 1993. However, there are one or two disasters Victor does not quite believe, such as rising damp, a plague of mice and a troublesome boxer - not forgetting the dreaded donkeys. Guest starring Joan Sims and Peter Cook

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The annual wine show is a major event in Brokenwood and no one is looking forward to it more than DI Mike, the recent buyer of a 10-acre vineyard. However, when a guest judge and wine critic are found dead, drowned inside a fermenting vat, the show takes second place to the murder investigation. Neill Rea, Fern Sutherland, Pana Hema Taylor and Nic Sampson star

programme.title: Miss Fisher's Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Phryne, Dot, Dr Mac and Aunt Prudence head to a picturesque holiday chalet to celebrate a white Christmas in July. However, they soon discover the body of one of the residents, who has apparently been electrocuted while hanging festive lights. When the death toll begins to rise, an unfortunate accident begins to look like a deliberate act of murder. Australian crime drama set in the 1920s, starring Essie Davis

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The detective investigates the murder of a university professor shot through the head while looking down his telescope. Meanwhile, Crabtree places an advert to locate his biological mother, and two women step forward claiming to be his parent. Drama, starring Yannick Bisson and Jonny Harris

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself on the trail of spies and a super-weapon as he searches for a missing boy. He grows close to the child's widowed mother as his investigation progresses - all of which puts a further strain on his personal relationship with Julia. Yannick Bisson

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: June's retirement party is put on hold when she and her ex-husband are taken hostage by the drugs gang. Rescue finally arrives and June faces the biggest decision of her life

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: An overworked Smithy loses his cool and accidentally reveals to a worried woman that her husband has been held on suspicion of stalking a prostitute. Michael Maloney guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Smithy encounters Ryan Jones again and finds the battered corpse of his wife Connie in the boot of his car. As the murderer makes his getaway in the London Underground, it soon becomes clear he has a personal vendetta against the Sun Hill desk sergeant and plans further killings, working his way through Smithy's address book - beginning with his mother Pauline. Michael Maloney guest stars

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Girl
programme.desc: Feature-length period drama, starring Siobhan Flynn. A dying woman takes her daughter to the home of her former lover, a wealthy mine agent, who takes the child in. But the man's wife is less than happy about the arrangement and determines to keep the girl apart from her own offspring. With Jonathan Cake, Mark Benton and Susan Jameson

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Tilly Trotter
programme.desc: Part one of two. Period drama about a 19-year-old girl who is attacked after a party celebrating the engagement of a farmer for whom she has feelings. Starring Carli Norris

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's Tilly Trotter
programme.desc: Concluding part. Following the destruction of their home by the McGraths, Tilly and her grandmother are forced to live a life of solitude. Tilly and Katie Drew begin working at Sopwith's mine. Despite the hardships of life in the pit, she enjoys feeling like part of the family. Period drama, starring Carli Norris and Sarah Jane Foster

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A water shortage means that the maternity home is busier than ever and two old schoolmates are reunited, but Marion shuns Shirley's attempts at renewing their friendship until they are brought together later in an unexpected way. Elsewhere, Barbara makes a revolutionary suggestion to help Sister Mary Cynthia treat the bedsores of her patient Tommy Mills, whose wife Gert may be more in need of care than he is. Fred thinks about taking the next step in his relationship with Violet. Drama, guest starring Una Stubbs and David Ryall

programme.title: In the Dark
programme.desc: As DI Helen Weeks grapples apprehensively with pregnancy, she is compelled to return to her loathed rural home town of Polesford, Derbyshire, with her partner and fellow detective Paul Hopkins. Two girls have been abducted and the man arrested is the husband of Helen's childhood best friend Linda Bates, who has been drawn into the centre of a media storm, while the local police force is under enormous pressure to get their man. But is he guilty? Crime drama adapted by Danny Brocklehurst from Mark Billingham's books, starring MyAnna Buring

programme.title: In the Dark
programme.desc: Helen and Paul are joined by pathologist friend Phil Hendricks as they become further embroiled in the abduction case. The clock is ticking to find Poppy alive and clear Bates's name, but to Paul's vexation, Helen seems increasingly disturbed by being in Polesford. Is she too distracted by pressing secrets past and present to keep her mind on the case? Crime drama, starring MyAnna Buring and Ben Batt

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Tony moves in with Deborah, while Gary and Dorothy decide to start a family, but the strain of finally growing up soon takes its toll on the foursome, not least Gary, who becomes overwhelmed by the prospect of fatherhood. Starring Neil Morrissey, Caroline Quentin and Leslie Ash

programme.title: Trial & Retribution
programme.desc: In a grim turn of events, the tooth of the abducted woman is found in the debris from a wood chipper, while Walker's ex-wife's boyfriend turns psychotic

programme.desc: Sarah leads the charge to rescue James, whose ordeal continues when his sexuality becomes known, and puts her relationship with George at risk when a disturbing discovery forces her to reveal her darkest secret

programme.desc: Arthur and Willy do battle with the authorities. Roy finally agrees to move in with Pat. Robbie turns vegetarian and Ted and Tony argue over the feasibility of Ted's new venture

Drama, starring Maggie O'Neill, Simon Shepherd and Gray O'Brien

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard is forced to think on his feet when he forgets his wedding anniversary and tells Hyacinth he has arranged to have a security alarm fitted as a present. Social-climbing comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans an extravagant riverside picnic and invites all and sundry from the higher echelons of society, including the vicar and his wife. Alas for her dreams of social success, the event proves a wash-out. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.desc: Smiler reminisces about a woman who escaped his advances during the war without leaving her contact details, so Truly, Alvin and Billy volunteer to track her down - even though it means a foray into hostile territory. Long-running comedy series, starring Frank Thornton, Brian Murphy, Keith Clifford and Stephen Lewis

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Tim Peake. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Chelsea v Leeds United and Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. After facing Liang Wenbo in the quarter-final, China's Ding Junhui played another of his compatriots at this stage last year, defeating Yan Bingtao to book his place in the final against Scot Stephen Maguire. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special 1990s edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tamara Beckwith, Saffron Sprackling, Clare Buckfield, John Pickard, Mr Motivator, Diane Youdale, Nick Moran and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Ali and Nevada from London, engaged couple Sean and Alan from Chester, and military drummer Ronnie and his partner Wesley from Aldershot

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: It's Musicals Week on Strictly, meaning that remaining contestants Bill, HRVY, Jamie, JJ, Maisie and Ranvir - minus Clara, who was voted off last week - are keen to dazzle as perform routines inspired by hit shows including Little Shop of Horrors, Phantom of the Opera, Chitty Chitty Bang Bang and A Chorus Line. However, it's also the quarter-final and with the final looming ever closer, the pressure is on. The extravaganza gets under way with a Priscilla, Queen of the Desert routine from the professionals, which features Gorka, Giovanni and Johannes dazzling in high heels and ever higher wigs. Presented Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Baltimore Ravens v Dallas Cowboys at M&T Bank Stadium

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Christmas traditions from three distinct parts of Britain. They begin by joining Advent preparations in the Highlands, meeting a Christmas tree farmer, a candlemaker and a woman who makes a traditional Scottish delicacy

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Rev Alan Miller leads a service for the Second Sunday of Advent from the Church of the Holy Rude in Stirling. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Scotland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at the Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about rare Faberge flowers

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley hosts the final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year competition as six girls and boys perform in front of judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson. Including a performance of O Holy Night by Aled Jones, Katherine Jenkins and all the finalists

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple up sticks from Birmingham and spend £500,000 on a country home that allows them to lead the good life while being close enough to Hereford for regular visits to their daughter.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best-loved festive movies

programme.title: Nativity 2: Danger in the Manger! (2012)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher enters his class in a Song for Christmas competition, and ends up having to lead the busload of excitable kids - as well as his irresponsible teaching assistant - on a disastrous trip to Wales for the event. The journey also leads to an unwanted confrontation with his high-flying twin brother. Comedy sequel, starring David Tennant, Marc Wootton, Joanna Page and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Sean Fletcher visit the Black Mountains, which straddle the border between Wales and England, to find out more about a drive to preserve ancient rural skills and traditions. Sean gets a bird's eye view of the spectacular landscape, while former thatcher Joe Sugg battles for supremacy in a hedge-laying competition. Joe Crowley investigates the challenges facing the Scottish salmon industry, and Adam Henson has high hopes for his new ram

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the two lowest-scoring couples face each other in the dance-off - with a place in next week's semi-final up for grabs. Musical theatre star Marisha Wallace performs Climb Ev'ry Mountain

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Will and Lyra plan to steal the alethiometer back from Boreal by cutting straight into his house from Cittagazze using the knife, while Mrs Coulter meets an intrigued Mary, who is later given a mysterious mission by the Cave. At the Magisterium, MacPhail condemns Graves to imprisonment

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following the life of award-winning writer from a young boy through to his early adult years. Having spent his childhood in a mostly white institutional care home with no love or family, Alex finally finds not only a sense of community for the first time in Brixton, but his identity and an ability to nurture his passion for music and DJing. When he is thrown in prison during the Brixton riots of 1981, he confronts his past and sees a path to healing. Starring Sheyi Cole, with Robbie Gee and Jonathan Jules, and written by Alastair Siddons and Steve McQueen, who also directed

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Anfield, Sheffield United v Leicester City at Bramall Lane and West Bromwich Albion v Crystal Palace at The Hawthorns

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Everton v Manchester City, Arsenal v Birmingham City and Chelsea v West Ham United

programme.title: StreetDance 2 (2012)
programme.desc: A dancer tries to put a humiliating defeat in a contest behind him by assembling an international dance crew to compete in a high-profile European event. He finds a perfect recruit in a Cuban salsa teacher, but his plan to combine their dance forms into a new style is threatened by their feelings for each other. Drama, starring Falk Hentschel, Tom Conti and Sofia Boutella

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin hear from the buyers of a bungalow in Langton Green, Kent, a house in Stoke-on-Trent and land in Northwood, north-west London

programme.title: Claimed and Shamed
programme.desc: A careless cyclist is left reeling after an incident with a bus. Elsewhere, a claimant tries to con an insurer with a fraudulent claim for lost jewellery

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Stories of bravery captured on phones, CCTV and dashcams, meeting those saved as well as the rescuers whose selfless acts can be the difference between life and death. In the first edition, CCTV captures the moment two boys are rescued from a rising tide and saved by a total stranger, while a community effort saves a seal being strangled to death by a rope around its neck.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items at the Newark Antiques Fair, while Charlie Ross finds out about an everyday object

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al is left kicking himself when he accidentally buys an engagement ring for Lily, while Bear is hyped up for a networking afternoon at the Mill, but learns an unexpected lesson

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which 30 recurring players compete for a daily prize of up to £1,500 and the chance to win £10,000. Every right answer takes them a step closer to a shot at that jackpot - but a single 'impossible' answer knocks them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple from Hampshire who are about to marry one another for the second time in their search for a property in Cornwall, and meets a local artist

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the recycling centre in Witley for items she can breathe new life into, taking away a tumble dryer drum, a record cabinet and a batch of wire mesh

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes on Serhat Ahmet as they search for items in the antiques shops of North Wales and the Midlands on their way to an auction in Bolton

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon returns to the pub to find Ian lying motionless on the floor and immediately calls Kathy. Unable to find a pulse, they panic as they wait for the ambulance to rush him to hospital, while the police are quickly on the scene and suspicions are running high. With so many locals having an axe to grind, who would want Ian dead?

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. Comedy, starring Dawn French, and Emma Chambers with a guest appearance by Kylie Minogue

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. Richard Curtis's comedy returns for three mini-episodes, starring Dawn French

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show from 2016, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Richard Osman, Tom Courtenay, Sara Pascoe and Chris Kamara. As usual, all participants will be hoping to deceive one another with plausible lies, such as Richard stating he has never seen Breaking Bad because of his Homes Under the Hammer addiction, Sir Tom claiming he enjoys whiskey on his Christmas morning cornflakes, and Chris explaining that his full name is actually Christmas Kamara. The team also presents a somewhat unusual take on the Nativity

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this 2019 spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a surprise departure from the staff, but there is also news of a new arrival at the salon

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: It is a big week for one of the team as she gets a long-awaited promotion, and also goes on her first-ever date, which does not turn out how she expected

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a break-in at the salon so Sammyjo and the girls turn detective, while Kallie's smelly staffroom habits become too much for the team

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Hannah Fry and Phil Wang joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actor and writer Stephen Fry, who is promoting Troy, his latest reworking of the Greek myths and Mamma Mia star Amanda Seyfried discusses new David Fincher film Mank. Back to the Future star and Parkinson's disease activist Michael J Fox chats about his memoir No Time Like the Future: An Optimist Considers Mortality, Nina Sosanya talks about her new Christmas drama, TV chef Jamie Oliver explains how his new book 7 Ways manages to breathe new life into staple ingredients and comedian Tom Allen talks about his memoir No Shame. Plus, Dolly Parton makes a special appearance performing the seaonal staple I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic great Hannah Cockcroft, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic hockey champion Kate Richardson-Walsh and Toronto Wolfpack forward Jon Wilkin join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker for the quiz

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson play video clips featuring a mysterious web that covered an entire street, and an Australian sea turned into a foam bath

programme.title: All Over the Place: Asia
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and his CBBC mates embark on a Vietnamese river adventure, where they make paper using an ancient technique and take part in a Bollywood extravaganza

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Mark Wright and Lauren Layfield create a wheelchair-accessible old-fashioned cinema den full of Hollywood glamour for 11-year-old movie buff Lola and her mother

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Children's magazine with information, topical items and performance

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: From slow-worms to foxes, naturalist Steve Backshall explores deadly animals found in gardens

programme.title: DIY Deadly with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Wildlife expert Steve Backshall turns detective to solve CBBC viewers' animal mysteries. From birds to bees, Steve answers questions about flying animals

programme.title: Waterhole: Africa's Animal Oasis
programme.desc: Waterholes are vital to the African ecosystem, but little is known about how they support so much life. Chris Packham and Ella Al-Shamahi work with Mwiba Wildlife Reserve in Tanzania to build the world's first waterhole with a built-in specialist camera rig

programme.title: Robot Wars: Battle of the Stars
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Angela Scanlon present the first of two specials in which celebrity teams led by the Brownlee brothers, Scott Mills, Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock and Suzi Perry design their dream robot with the help of an experienced engineer, then battle it out in the arena - where all the same rules apply for the celebrities as they face off against each other and the imposing house robots. Who will become the Celebrity Robot Wars champions?

programme.title: 12 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Woody the St Bernard moves in with five-year-old Dexter, who has cerebral palsy. Dexter's family, in Surrey, are hoping that the little pup will become his companion and learn to carry out simple assistance tasks. In Hull, Mungo joins Alastair, Daron and their possessive older cocker spaniel Milo - but the dogs clash from day one, and Alastair and Daron call on Louise to help

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King create festive recipes and chat to celebrity guests, with the Rev Kate Bottley helping them make cherry and bourbon-glazed gammon

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and the teams search for well-priced antiques and collectibles to sell at auction in the streets of Hungerford, Berkshire, with the assistance of experts Philip Serrell and Richard Madley

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore saves three things about to be dumped at a tip, transforms them into bespoke and valuable pieces, before selling them and giving the profits to the people who were getting rid of them in the first place

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson is in Hampshire with a dynamic couple who love the outdoors and have £650,000 to buy a property and, while in the county, visits the Solent Sky Museum

programme.title: The Railway Children (1970)
programme.desc: Three Edwardian children are forced to leave their comfortable middle-class London home and move to the wilds of Yorkshire after their father is wrongfully imprisoned for treason. Family drama adapted from E Nesbit's story, starring Jenny Agutter, Sally Thomsett, Gary Warren, Dinah Sheridan, Bernard Cribbins, William Mervyn and Iain Cuthbertson

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents further coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. This is the first time the competition has been held outside York since 2010, when John Higgins prevailed in Telford. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Porridge (1979)
programme.desc: Cellmates Fletcher and Godber are coerced into aiding an escape attempt organised by the sinister Grouty. However, the two jailbirds contrive to break back into prison before their absence is detected by the formidable Mr Mackay. Comedy based on the TV series, starring Ronnie Barker, Richard Beckinsale, Peter Vaughan, Fulton Mackay and Brian Wilde

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. Scotland's Stephen Maguire was in dominant form at this stage last year, as he thrashed Northern Ireland's Mark Allen 6-0 to reach the final of this tournament for the third time in his career and the first since 2007. However, as was the case 12 years earlier, he did not manage to add to his 2004 title, losing to Ding Junhui in the showpiece match. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Dirty God (2019)
programme.desc: Jade, a young mother in the prime of her life, is left severely burned by an acid attack. While her face has been reconstructed, her beauty is lost beneath the scars. Descending a self-destructive path with relationships crumbling, Jade must take drastic action to reclaim her life. Drama, starring Vicky Knight and Katherine Kelly

programme.title: Louis Theroux: My Scientology Movie (2015)
programme.desc: The reporter investigates the controversial religious organisation, but finds himself denied access to anyone involved. Adopting a different approach, he resorts to recruiting actors to re-enact the personal stories of former members - only to find his project is under surveillance by representatives of the church

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: Glorious Gardens from Above
programme.desc: Wales is the destination for this part of Christine Walkden's balloon tour of some of Britain's finest gardens. She learns how to brace an ancient yew in the grounds of Powis Castle, and helps restore an obscured view at the Dingle Garden. Plus, the story of Mona Holloway, a wartime land girl who found love on a neighbouring farm

programme.title: Life in a Cottage Garden with Carol Klein
programme.desc: In May and June, the horticulturist plants sweet peas, picks the year's first salad leaves and stakes perennials into the ground. She also witnesses the blossoming of bluebells, primal ferns and oriental poppies, before she and her husband Neil take delivery of their first hive of honey bees

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond discovers how mountain resorts are preparing for the winter season, Arlene Stuart meets the Argyll community rewilding their sea loch with native oysters and Euan McIlwraith investigates how clear labelling is on Scottish-produced beef. Plus, Professor Hayden Lorimer rediscovers another lost construction in the landscape, and this week it's one man's folly

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: The work of two community gardening groups - the Easthaven Community, whose plan to make their village square mode attractive developed into an award-winning story of regeneration, and the Dunkeld vegetable growing initiative The Field, which now grows produce on a huge scale

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Rick Stein's Cornish Christmas
programme.desc: Part one of two. The chef celebrates Christmas in his adopted county of Cornwall, exploring the music, food and drink of the region as he takes a break from his worldwide travel adventures. As well as joining in the ancient tradition of wassailing, he prepares a goose, tests out the ice rink at Eden and enjoys the singing of Port Isaac's Fisherman's Friends

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The cook and food writer celebrates well-loved festive dishes, cooking a sea bass starter, a roast duck feast and a party trifle. In addition to preparing dishes inspired by memories of his family, he gives a makeover to his father's annual contribution to Christmas dinner - a casserole. He also creates a winter slaw inspired by the Slather holiday cheeseboard, and accepts an invitation to venison supper in a Scottish castle, where he invents a new cocktail to toast the special occasion

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening session of the final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 19 frames. China's Ding Junhui met Scotland's Stephen Maguire in last year's showpiece match at the Barbican Centre in York, and Ding took a 5-3 lead during the afternoon session, including breaks of 105 and 128. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The valuation day venue is Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, where experts James Lewis and Adam Partridge are out in force to find the best antiques and collectibles. Plus, Paul Martin finds out about the legendary Tom Fool, the court jester who lived at the castle and caused mayhem during some of its most turbulent times

programme.title: Inside the Factory
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace visits a mattress factory in Leeds, where he discovers how steel is stretched and coiled into springs and the advantages of natural fibres like hemp and wool. Cherry Healey talks to a sleep scientist about the benefits to reaction time from taking an afternoon nap, and Ruth Goodman tries out a Middle Ages mattress made out of a straw-stuffed sack

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the concluding session of the final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 19 frames. Ding Junhui of China took the trophy in 2019, defeating Scot Stephen Maguire 10-6 and hitting four century breaks, including 131 in the penultimate frame. With this victory, his first triumph in the event since 2009, Ding became the fifth person to have won this title three or more times. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: One Man and His Shoes
programme.desc: Documentary about the social, cultural and racial impact of Air Jordan sneakers, revealing how groundbreaking marketing strategies created a multi-billion-dollar business with a dark side. Manufacturer Nike's high price tag and marketing strategy has fuelled a demand so high that since 1989, young people have killed and been killed for a pair of the famous sports shoes

programme.title: Louis Theroux's Weird Weekends
programme.desc: The intrepid reporter meets some strange and colourful characters on his way to Christmas dinner in America, including porn star JJ Michael, UFO obsessed Rev Robert Short and aspiring folk singer Mike Oehler

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Moments after shots ring out, the identity of Nathan's assassin and the reasons why the Texas conference had to be cut short are revealed. Peter and Matt are thrown into unexpected and uncharted territory, while Sylar decides a visit to Claire could give him a boost. Elsewhere, Hiro and Ando are charged with safeguarding a dangerous family secret and Suresh makes a breakthrough that could change the world

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Sylar, freshly armed with his formidable arsenal of abilities, wages a one-man war against the Company's main facility - but his explorations of the mysterious Level Five reveal more than he bargained for. Meanwhile, Angela clashes with a much-changed Peter over how to avert a worldwide catastrophe and Claire discovers something unexpected about her abilities. Zachary Quinto and Milo Ventimiglia star

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from a virtual audience on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha makes a controversial decision while operating on a cancerous patient, which leaves Max, Donna and Dom all concerned about his actions and general welfare - but their interference results in him taking out his frustrations on Jodie. Cameron tries to get back on a furious Chloe's good side, but his actions have disastrous consequences for a patient. Clayton demands that Kian come clean about his drug habit

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles steps back in time to the 'Georgian days', revisiting the show's episodes in which six teams bought some of the best Georgian items. Eric takes a look at how the items sold at auction, and shares some top tips on what viewers should look out for when scouring the fairs

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Newly qualified vet Patrick is called out to help a farmer whose cow has been struggling all night with a difficult labour. Patrick discovers her calf is breached, and has to reposition it. However, once it's in the correct position, a shock discovery makes his job twice as precarious. Back in Castlewellan, a farmer arrives with a two-week-old lamb that's developed a limp. Some antibiotics and a dose of anti-inflammatories will help the lamb back on its feet. However, vet Maeve notices its eyes are milky white and she has to use all her expertise and knowledge to save the lamb's sight

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary explores how families use food to create their own sense of home, including a father and daughter who have taken their home cooking to the streets with their own food van. Later in the programme, Mary makes some of her own family favourites, including a hearty bolognaise bake with a burrata and heritage tomato salad, and a paneer and roast vegetable curry

programme.title: The Week in Parliament
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's proceedings in Parliament, presented by David Cornock

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Head Hunters
programme.desc: Today's Head Hunters are first-timer and civil servant Roisin, three-time Head Hunter Callum, and customer advisor Dubem, who has been on every show this week

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Home for Christmas
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers create festive dishes with the help of The One Show's Alex Jones, including a turkey salad, figs stuffed with stilton, and a chocolate and chestnut roulade

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The cook demonstrates how to create meals using Christmas dinner leftovers, ensuring nothing bought during the most expensive food shopping trip of the year needs go to waste. He also gets his garden ready for a new year of fruit and vegetable growing, before visiting local allotments and the kitchen plot at Chatsworth House in Derbyshire to show how to make the most of seasonal produce

programme.title: The Belles of St Trinian's (1954)
programme.desc: The mischievous schoolgirls hope to get rich quick by betting on a racehorse belonging to a classmate's wealthy father. However, they first have to foil a gang of thieves planning to steal the animal and outwit an undercover policewoman sent to infiltrate the school disguised as a teacher. Comedy, starring Alastair Sim, Joyce Grenfell and George Cole

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: David Attenborough examines how fleas are able to jump great distances for their size and attempts to discover how fast a cheetah can run. He also investigates the surprising preventive and curative 'medicine' developed by certain species, including the hippopotamus, which secretes a red slime to act as a highly efficient sun screen

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and Anita Manning are at Culzean Castle in South Ayrshire, where they assess items including a selection of golf memorabilia and a trio of Moorcroft ceramics

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Alex Jones, Jessica Knappet, Rob Rinder and Karim Zeroual go test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: Strictly: It Takes Two
programme.desc: Fanzine devoted to the ballroom dancing show, featuring an interview with the latest couple to be voted off, as well as behind-the-scenes footage of rehearsals

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: John Humphrys invites four more contestants to brave the black chair to answer questions on the specialist subjects of freshwater fish of the British Isles, Nick Cave, the history of scouting in the UK, and Lin-Manuel Miranda, before they are tested on their general knowledge

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts as two teams who lost their first-round matches get another chance to stay in the competition and reach the second stage. The contestants must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues asking what connects a small drink in Armenia, desire in Japan, achieved victory in South Korea and a bell sound in Vietnam

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson's grandmother's recipe for creme caramel provides the inspiration for a simple but elegant dessert of caramel custard. She then rifles through her pasta collection to find the ideal shape to go with her spicy sauce made from Italian 'nduja and cavolo nero, followed by a visit to the gadget cupboard for an electric potato masher to prepare her brown butter colcannon, the perfect match to a dish of black pudding meatballs in tomato sauce.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The second round of the student quiz continues, with two more teams of four pitting their wits against one another as they attempt to secure a place in the quarter-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions

programme.title: Hospital
programme.desc: A look at cutting-edge treatments being used by clinicians to fight Covid-19, including a 72-year-old intensive care patient is given an experimental drug in the hope that it will improve his lung function. A woman who needs to recover from the virus quickly to have a life-saving mastectomy is randomly selected to receive convalescent plasma, which is harvested from the blood of those who have already had coronavirus

programme.title: The Noughties
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon is joined by Cherry Healey and Tyger Drew-Honey to take a look at the year's cultural highlights, including the first glimpse of new singing talent Adele on Jools Holland's Later, the Spice Girls reuniting, and Shilpa Shetty on the receiving end of racist abuse from her housemates in Celebrity Big Brother

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: The Schools That Chain Boys
programme.desc: An investigation by BBC News Arabic uncovering systemic child abuse inside Islamic schools in Sudan, finding that boys as young as five are routinely chained, shackled and beaten

programme.title: Thanks for the Memories
programme.desc: Part one of two. Joyce is happy with her life, but everything is turned on its head when an accident requires a blood transfusion and she wakes up with memories that are not hers. Romantic comedy based on a novel by Cecelia Ahern, starring Tom Wlaschiha and Tamzin Merchant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: The team launches Plant Britain, an ambitious two-year challenge aimed at encouraging everyone in the nation to get planting - no matter where they live or how much space they have. Matt Baker is in Cheshire planting the very first trees in what will become the programmes Plant Britain wood, while Anita Rani is in her hometown of Bradford to help residents of the Canterbury estate to plant trees in their front gardens and at a local primary school

programme.title: My Family, the Holocaust & Me with Robert Rinder
programme.desc: Part one of two. The barrister helps the second and third generations of British Jewish families affected by the Holocaust to retrace their relatives' footsteps and discover the full truth about what happened to them. Robert begins by meeting two British Jewish families who want to investigate what happened to their relatives, as well as exploring his own family's stories on both his mother's and his father's sides

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The chefs are challenged to fillet and smoke a trout for their Skills Test, and serve the results with celeriac remoulade. Their next task is to make a classic crepe suzette, a dish that requires extreme precision, while in the final round, they must impress judges Marcus Wareing, Gregg Wallace and Monica Galetti with their very own two course signature dishes

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto concludes his travels in the south of Italy, where his family has lived for 500 years. He explores the rock caves of Matera, attends Nocera's Easter celebrations and finds out about collapsible houses designed to help the residents avoid paying taxes. He also visits Sicily, where he takes a trip to Mount Etna and the catacombs of Palermo

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the unflappable traveller visits Mexico, the USA and Canada. He roams the frozen wastes of Alaska, pauses at the Mexican border, does time in Alcatraz, hangs out with lumberjacks and hitches a lift with the US Coast Guard as he tries to get back to the exact spot from where he started all those miles before

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: The team edges nearer to identifying a suspect with the help of a fingerprint lifted from Thor's photograph and CCTV footage from the shopping mall, but this success is jeopardised by a leak to social media that risks putting further lives in danger. In Icelandic

programme.title: The Valhalla Murders
programme.desc: Kata is suspended from duty for losing her gun, while Arnar questions the hospitalised Steinthor, who strenuously denies killing his friend Tomas. Kata gets an intriguing phone call from Hakon, who has discovered something in the basement at Valhalla. In Icelandic

programme.title: Operation Iceberg
programme.desc: Part two of two. Chris Packham, Helen Czerski, Andy Torbet, Chris van Tulleken and Doug Allan continue their adventures in the Arctic, boarding an unpredictable iceberg to determine whether the impact of waves on the ice or the warming temperature of the surrounding water causes them to decay. Andy and Doug dive into the sea, medic Chris Van Tulleken looks at the physiological reactions of the human body in extreme immersion and Chris Packham takes to the skies to conduct a census documenting just how many polar bears could be living on the berg

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: Pressure mounts as Tommy's uncooperative mother cannot be held much longer without the supply of evidence. Niels Thormod's name resurfaces in the investigation as it transpires a private detective he hired hoping to find something more than the police has gone missing. Lillian is hard-pressed to solve the case and it seems there is an inside source leaking information. Saga is called to Sweden when a young man wanting to confess to killing his father, has a connection to Henrik's family's past. Crime drama, in Danish and Swedish, starring Sofia Helin and Thure Lindhardt

programme.title: The Bridge
programme.desc: The homeless girls discover the identity of the killer following an encounter with psychologist Niels Thormod, and try to get in touch with Henrik to pass on what they know. However, they are forced to flee for their lives when the murderer comes looking for them. Saga finds a match for the killer's fingerprint and seems to have the case solved. But as she sets out to finally bring him to justice, a last minute revelation involving a seemingly insignificant detail changes everything. In Danish and Swedish

programme.title: Full Circle with Michael Palin
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the unflappable traveller visits Mexico, the USA and Canada. He roams the frozen wastes of Alaska, pauses at the Mexican border, does time in Alcatraz, hangs out with lumberjacks and hitches a lift with the US Coast Guard as he tries to get back to the exact spot from where he started all those miles before

programme.title: Francesco's Italy Top to Toe
programme.desc: Francesco da Mosto concludes his travels in the south of Italy, where his family has lived for 500 years. He explores the rock caves of Matera, attends Nocera's Easter celebrations and finds out about collapsible houses designed to help the residents avoid paying taxes. He also visits Sicily, where he takes a trip to Mount Etna and the catacombs of Palermo

programme.title: West Coast Otters
programme.desc: Portrait of two otters living on the idyllic west coast of Scotland. An intimate relationship between the inseparable mother and daughter is observed. The cub is seen learning to fend for herself and, as she faces the dangers of her first Scottish winter, mum must work hard to ensure they both survive. Narrated by Pam Ferris

programme.title: Addicted to Sheep
programme.desc: A year in the life of a family of North Pennines tenant hill farmers. As Tom and Kay Hutchinson work through sun, rain, sleet and snow to try to breed the perfect sheep, their three children attend the local primary school, which is comprised entirely of farmers' children

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Tristan decides to take up bell-ringing when he learns it is a favourite pastime of the attractive new schoolmistress. Meanwhile, Siegfried becomes nervous when an enormous wolfhound needs treatment. Peter Davison, Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy star in a feature-length episode from 1990, marking an end to the rural drama

programme.title: The Cult of Sunday Night
programme.desc: Strand examining the appeal of Sunday night shows, beginning with a look at All Creatures Great and Small which could be said to have set the template for such programmes with its mix of nostalgia, romance, eccentric characters and stunning scenery. However, the cast and crew reveal things were often much less cosy behind the scenes, thanks to accidents, cantankerous actors and the limited supply of James Herriot books. Narrated by Meera Syal

programme.title: The Workshop (2017)
programme.desc: Olivia, a well-known Parisian novelist, runs a writing workshop with a group of young people in the South of France. She's fascinated by unsociable pupil Antoine, and they become absorbed by an extreme right-wing ideology, while developing a relationship that is marked by attraction and repulsion. Drama, starring Marina Fois and Matthieu Lucci. In French

programme.title: Andrew Davies: Rewriting the Classics
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the creative process of the screenwriter behind some of television's most acclaimed adaptations of classic literature, from the 1995 version of Pride and Prejudice to the BBC's new dramatisation of Victor Hugo's Les Miserables. He also discusses how to go about transforming sprawling, lengthy and detailed novels into primetime drama, turning one art form into another altogether different one. With contributions from authors Sarah Waters and Helen Fielding, actors Dominic West and Olivia Colman, and industry figures Jane Tranter and Sue Birtwhistle

programme.title: Sheila Hancock Brushes Up: The Art of Watercolours
programme.desc: The actress presents a history of watercolour painting, discovering a catalogue of little-known works and exploring how the medium's immediacy and freedom has given it an enduring appeal for both amateur and professional artists. She visits locations including the Alps, India and Venice to tell the stories behind paintings that were created there, as well as examining the importance of watercolours to artists in the days before photography

programme.title: The Cult of Sunday Night
programme.desc: Strand examining the appeal of Sunday night shows, beginning with a look at All Creatures Great and Small which could be said to have set the template for such programmes with its mix of nostalgia, romance, eccentric characters and stunning scenery. However, the cast and crew reveal things were often much less cosy behind the scenes, thanks to accidents, cantankerous actors and the limited supply of James Herriot books. Narrated by Meera Syal

programme.title: Addicted to Sheep
programme.desc: A year in the life of a family of North Pennines tenant hill farmers. As Tom and Kay Hutchinson work through sun, rain, sleet and snow to try to breed the perfect sheep, their three children attend the local primary school, which is comprised entirely of farmers' children

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: Christmas University Challenge
programme.desc: Teams of distinguished graduates compete on behalf of their former universities in the academic quiz's festive incarnation, with the opening first-round match seeing the University of Leeds taking on Clare College, Cambridge. Among those facing questions from host Jeremy Paxman are broadcaster the Rev Richard Coles and photographer Tim Allen for Leeds, while the Clare team includes writer Marcel Theroux and soprano Elin Manahan Thomas

programme.title: The Art Mysteries with Waldemar Januszczak
programme.desc: The art critic examines some of the art world's biggest enigmas, beginning with Van Gogh's Self-Portrait with Bandaged Ear, asking why the painter cut off his ear

programme.title: Mary Beard's Shock of the Nude
programme.desc: Part two of two. The classicist looks at an eclectic range of works to show how artists have challenged the idea of 'the body beautiful', and explores how artistic nudes can provoke viewers to consider fundamental questions about being human. Mary reflects on Marc Quinn's sculpture of artist Alison Lapper, Sally Mann's images of her own children in the 1990s and the controversy that surrounded their exhibition, and examines society's shifting perception of gender, from the binary of male and female to a more fluid concept

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Documentary following the story of a joint venture between the Red Army and Pittsburgh Penguins ice hockey teams shortly after the collapse of the Soviet Union. Eccentric marketing whiz Steven Warshaw was sent to Russia and given the task of transforming the joint teams into the greatest show in Moscow at a pivotal moment in US-Russia relations, when oligarchs made their fortunes and multiple murders went unsolved

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Stella Rimington examines British perceptions of Russia over the past 150 years. The former director general of MI5 journeys to the East End of London in search of Russian revolutionaries and Chopwell, a town once known for its mining that was dubbed 'Little Moscow'. Rimington also talks to former Soviet dissident Vladimir Bukovsky and ex-British ambassador Rodric Braithwaite

programme.title: Russia 1917: Countdown to Revolution
programme.desc: A look at Russia's transition from tsarist autocracy to communist state, focusing on the involvement of three men - Lenin, Trotsky and Stalin, all of whom emerged from obscurity to forge an entirely new political system in the space of a few months. This drama-documentary examines the 245 days that brought these men to supreme power. As the history unfolds writers and historians, including Martin Amis, Orlando Figes, Helen Rappaport, Simon Sebag Montefiore and China Mieville, battle over the meaning of the Russian Revolution and explore how it shaped the world of today. Narrated by Juliet Stevenson

programme.title: Bob Ross: The Happy Painter
programme.desc: Documentary taking a behind-the-scenes look at American painter Bob Ross's journey from humble beginnings to pop culture icon, charting how one local TV commercial evolved into a viral phenomenon that continues to inspire millions around the world. The film reveals the public and private sides of Bob Ross through accounts from close friends and family, childhood photographs and rare archive footage. Interviewees recount his gentle, mild-mannered demeanour and unwavering dedication to wildlife as well as disclosing little-known facts about his famous curly hair - which was naturally straight - and his fascination with fast cars

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: What is it like when your mum becomes your dad? Twelve-year-old Tilly’s mum came out as transgender when she was 7 years old. We follow Tilly as she shows us what her life is like now. Having just moved up to secondary school, Tilly hasn't told her new friends about her family as she is uncertain as to when will be the right time to tell them. When her birthday party comes up, Tilly has a decision to make - will she explain to her friends that Jack is trans or will she make up a story to buy herself more time? To help her decide, Tilly meets Nat and Faith, who both have trans dads and help her feel more confident about sharing her story. And when younger brother Mal experiences some mean comments at school, new friend Nat offers to give a talk to his class.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Lars and Kyan swim through the shallows of Crystal River in Florida. Kyan tests his new swimming fin, while Lars watches a manatee and her baby cruise by. Xuli is water-skiing further downstream in the open waters of Crystal Bay, while Foz and Ubercorn watch on from Jet Pad. Grandmaster Glitch is taking the tourist family on a mermaid spotting tour of Crystal River aboard his Grimmer-boat. As Glitch heads off in search of real mermaids, he accidentally startles the mother manatee. Ubercorn realises the manatee will be in danger from the speeding boats and from the cold deep water. The Go Jetters need to mount a manatee rescue quickly. While Xuli attempts to stop the speedboats, Foz launches the Vroomster. The baby manatee has become attached to Kyan, so Lars suggests he remains with her, while Lars and Foz go to save the mother. Kyan is not happy at having to play baby-sitter.

programme.desc: It’s Frosty Frolics Day, and Love Monster plans to spend it with Bad Idea Puppy and his cousin Good Idea Puppy, who has come to visit. Love Monster thinks that since Good Idea Puppy is the guest, she should choose which activity they do first. She decides that they should start with the ice slide. Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy go together and have a great time, but Bad Idea Puppy has to go on his own and struggles to slide down. After the slide, they visit the Frosty Frolics Fayre, and Good Idea Puppy and Love Monster accidentally choose activities made for two all day long. Finally, just as Love Monster and Good Idea Puppy are about to go on a tandem ski through the forest, Bad Idea Puppy asks if this time he can go with his cousin so he won’t be the odd puppy out. Love Monster happily agrees, but soon he realises that frosty frolics aren’t fun on your own, and that Bad Idea Puppy must have been feeling left out all day.
