programme.desc: Maddie learns how a radio telescope collects radio-waves and finds out how a maze is made.

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Captain Captain is surprised to find Cook and Line on their best behaviour, after they become tired of losing games, and decide to be good, like the Swashbucklers

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the Squirrels go on a mission to contain an outbreak.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Tommy Brock decides he has had enough of the cold, so he takes shelter by the fire inside the Bouncer's burrow

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: CBeebies' motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that go off-road

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to South America in search of an armadillo. Along the way, he bumps into a pair of squabbling guanaco and meets a family of mara, a type of giant rodent

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Children's programme.

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Glitch causes catastrophe on Whistler Mountain in Canada in a cable car hanging from a wire stretching three kilometres across the valley, higher than the Eiffel Tower

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb and his friends are snowed in at the nursery on Christmas Eve, so they decide to have a sleepover. Timmy waits eagerly for Santa to appear, but struggles to keep his eyes open as tiredness sets in

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Revival of the classic animation about the adventures of a clan of friendly aliens living on a small blue planet. Michael Palin narrates

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy thinks the ball is tired of bouncing, so she decides to give it a rest

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Bernard Cribbins reads One Winter's Night by Claire Freedman and Simon Mendez

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Tim Peake. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Chelsea v Leeds United and Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. After facing Liang Wenbo in the quarter-final, China's Ding Junhui played another of his compatriots at this stage last year, defeating Yan Bingtao to book his place in the final against Scot Stephen Maguire. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special 1990s edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tamara Beckwith, Saffron Sprackling, Clare Buckfield, John Pickard, Mr Motivator, Diane Youdale, Nick Moran and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Ali and Nevada from London, engaged couple Sean and Alan from Chester, and military drummer Ronnie and his partner Wesley from Aldershot

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: It's Musicals Week on Strictly, meaning that remaining contestants Bill, HRVY, Jamie, JJ, Maisie and Ranvir - minus Clara, who was voted off last week - are keen to dazzle as perform routines inspired by hit shows including Little Shop of Horrors, Phantom of the Opera, Chitty Chitty Bang Bang and A Chorus Line. However, it's also the quarter-final and with the final looming ever closer, the pressure is on. The extravaganza gets under way with a Priscilla, Queen of the Desert routine from the professionals, which features Gorka, Giovanni and Johannes dazzling in high heels and ever higher wigs. Presented Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Baltimore Ravens v Dallas Cowboys at M&T Bank Stadium

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: All the latest political news from Wales and beyond, presented by James Williams

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Christmas traditions from three distinct parts of Britain. They begin by joining Advent preparations in the Highlands, meeting a Christmas tree farmer, a candlemaker and a woman who makes a traditional Scottish delicacy

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Rev Alan Miller leads a service for the Second Sunday of Advent from the Church of the Holy Rude in Stirling. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Scotland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at the Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about rare Faberge flowers

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley hosts the final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year competition as six girls and boys perform in front of judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson. Including a performance of O Holy Night by Aled Jones, Katherine Jenkins and all the finalists

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best-loved festive movies

programme.title: Nativity 2: Danger in the Manger! (2012)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher enters his class in a Song for Christmas competition, and ends up having to lead the busload of excitable kids - as well as his irresponsible teaching assistant - on a disastrous trip to Wales for the event. The journey also leads to an unwanted confrontation with his high-flying twin brother. Comedy sequel, starring David Tennant, Marc Wootton, Joanna Page and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway leaves his walking boots behind for once as he soars over the old slate quarry in Bethesda on the new zip wire there. He is accompanied by Afghan writer Homan Yousofi, who has made his home in north Wales after studying at Bangor University, and together they explore the landscape around the town of Bethesda, walking past the old mines and then on to the spectacular glacial valley of Nant Ffrancon. For his second walk, Derek and illustrator Anne Cakebread set off from the riverside village of St Dogmaels in Pembrokeshire, heading along the Teifi estuary and climbing the clifftop path above Cemaes Head

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen looks at how people get from A to B in Wales, featuring archive clips of motorcycle stunts, runaway buses and giant inflatables

programme.title: Wales and the History of the World
programme.desc: Eddie Butler explores the involvement of Wales in different wars through the ages, from the Roman invasion to the Second World War. He also learns about the country's contribution to battlefield medicine, how Spanish children ended up exiled in Bangor and why there is a replica of a French guillotine in Welshpool

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Sean Fletcher visit the Black Mountains, which straddle the border between Wales and England, to find out more about a drive to preserve ancient rural skills and traditions. Sean gets a bird's eye view of the spectacular landscape, while former thatcher Joe Sugg battles for supremacy in a hedge-laying competition. Joe Crowley investigates the challenges facing the Scottish salmon industry, and Adam Henson has high hopes for his new ram

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the two lowest-scoring couples face each other in the dance-off - with a place in next week's semi-final up for grabs. Musical theatre star Marisha Wallace performs Climb Ev'ry Mountain

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Will and Lyra plan to steal the alethiometer back from Boreal by cutting straight into his house from Cittagazze using the knife, while Mrs Coulter meets an intrigued Mary, who is later given a mysterious mission by the Cave. At the Magisterium, MacPhail condemns Graves to imprisonment

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following the life of award-winning writer from a young boy through to his early adult years. Having spent his childhood in a mostly white institutional care home with no love or family, Alex finally finds not only a sense of community for the first time in Brixton, but his identity and an ability to nurture his passion for music and DJing. When he is thrown in prison during the Brixton riots of 1981, he confronts his past and sees a path to healing. Starring Sheyi Cole, with Robbie Gee and Jonathan Jules, and written by Alastair Siddons and Steve McQueen, who also directed

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Anfield, Sheffield United v Leicester City at Bramall Lane and West Bromwich Albion v Crystal Palace at The Hawthorns

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Everton v Manchester City, Arsenal v Birmingham City and Chelsea v West Ham United

programme.title: StreetDance 2 (2012)
programme.desc: A dancer tries to put a humiliating defeat in a contest behind him by assembling an international dance crew to compete in a high-profile European event. He finds a perfect recruit in a Cuban salsa teacher, but his plan to combine their dance forms into a new style is threatened by their feelings for each other. Drama, starring Falk Hentschel, Tom Conti and Sofia Boutella

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Sally Nugent present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Kym Marsh and Gethin Jones host the weekday magazine show featuring a mixture of topical chat and expert advice

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin hear from the buyers of a bungalow in Langton Green, Kent, a house in Stoke-on-Trent and land in Northwood, north-west London

programme.title: Saved on Camera
programme.desc: Stories of bravery captured on phones, CCTV and dashcams, meeting those saved as well as the rescuers whose selfless acts can be the difference between life and death. In the first edition, CCTV captures the moment two boys are rescued from a rising tide and saved by a total stranger, while a community effort saves a seal being strangled to death by a rope around its neck.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and David Harper are in Nottinghamshire to help two teams look for items at the Newark Antiques Fair, while Charlie Ross finds out about an everyday object

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al is left kicking himself when he accidentally buys an engagement ring for Lily, while Bear is hyped up for a networking afternoon at the Mill, but learns an unexpected lesson

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which 30 recurring players compete for a daily prize of up to £1,500 and the chance to win £10,000. Every right answer takes them a step closer to a shot at that jackpot - but a single 'impossible' answer knocks them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Ginny Buckley helps a couple from Hampshire who are about to marry one another for the second time in their search for a property in Cornwall, and meets a local artist

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore scours the recycling centre in Witley for items she can breathe new life into, taking away a tumble dryer drum, a record cabinet and a batch of wire mesh

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion takes on Serhat Ahmet as they search for items in the antiques shops of North Wales and the Midlands on their way to an auction in Bolton

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Hosted by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Sharon returns to the pub to find Ian lying motionless on the floor and immediately calls Kathy. Unable to find a pulse, they panic as they wait for the ambulance to rush him to hospital, while the police are quickly on the scene and suspicions are running high. With so many locals having an axe to grind, who would want Ian dead?

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: Geraldine is keen to find a celebrity to open the autumn fair in style - and Alice inadvertently comes up with the ideal candidate by letting slip she is related to one Reg Dwight - AKA Elton John. Comedy, starring Dawn French, and Emma Chambers with a guest appearance by Kylie Minogue

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. Richard Curtis's comedy returns for three mini-episodes, starring Dawn French

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show from 2016, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Richard Osman, Tom Courtenay, Sara Pascoe and Chris Kamara. As usual, all participants will be hoping to deceive one another with plausible lies, such as Richard stating he has never seen Breaking Bad because of his Homes Under the Hammer addiction, Sir Tom claiming he enjoys whiskey on his Christmas morning cornflakes, and Chris explaining that his full name is actually Christmas Kamara. The team also presents a somewhat unusual take on the Nativity

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this 2019 spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a surprise departure from the staff, but there is also news of a new arrival at the salon

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: It is a big week for one of the team as she gets a long-awaited promotion, and also goes on her first-ever date, which does not turn out how she expected

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a break-in at the salon so Sammyjo and the girls turn detective, while Kallie's smelly staffroom habits become too much for the team

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Hannah Fry and Phil Wang joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actor and writer Stephen Fry, who is promoting Troy, his latest reworking of the Greek myths and Mamma Mia star Amanda Seyfried discusses new David Fincher film Mank. Back to the Future star and Parkinson's disease activist Michael J Fox chats about his memoir No Time Like the Future: An Optimist Considers Mortality, Nina Sosanya talks about her new Christmas drama, TV chef Jamie Oliver explains how his new book 7 Ways manages to breathe new life into staple ingredients and comedian Tom Allen talks about his memoir No Shame. Plus, Dolly Parton makes a special appearance performing the seaonal staple I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic great Hannah Cockcroft, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic hockey champion Kate Richardson-Walsh and Toronto Wolfpack forward Jon Wilkin join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker for the quiz

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The UK's weather is becoming more extreme, with unprecedented rainfall, sunshine and sustained high temperatures during 2020. In this programme, Justin Rowlatt visits communities around Britain battered by this year's extreme weather to find out how they have coped. With access to Met Office data and experts explaining how hot and wet every part of the UK could become, he discovers a future of more heatwaves, intense storms and little snow for most of the nation, and asks whether Britain is ready for the even wilder weather that is coming

programme.title: EastEnders

Unable to find a pulse, they panic as they wait for the ambulance to rush him to hospital, while the police are quickly on the scene and suspicions are running high. With so many locals having an axe to grind, who would want Ian dead?

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: In Perthshire and Central Scotland, the judges visit a home inspired by Vikings, a converted ruined steading, and a maritime-themed fisherman's cottage

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. Richard Curtis's comedy returns for three mini-episodes, starring Dawn French

programme.title: Stormborn
programme.desc: The lands on the northern edges of the Atlantic bloom as nearly 24 hours of daylight transforms the snow-clad landscapes. Arctic fox clans struggle to raise their cubs on Iceland's volcanic shores, while on Shetland an otter cub strikes out alone, and a lone puffin searches for a partner. Ewan McGregor narrates

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a surprise departure from the staff, but there is also news of a new arrival at the salon

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: It is a big week for one of the team as she gets a long-awaited promotion, and also goes on her first-ever date, which does not turn out how she expected

programme.title: Angels of the North
programme.desc: There is a break-in at the salon so Sammyjo and the girls turn detective, while Kallie's smelly staffroom habits become too much for the team

programme.title: The Edit
programme.desc: The best of the week's entertainment news with Amy Irons

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts an extended edition of the satirical quiz, with Hannah Fry and Phil Wang joining Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the week's events

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: The host greets actor and writer Stephen Fry, who is promoting Troy, his latest reworking of the Greek myths and Mamma Mia star Amanda Seyfried discusses new David Fincher film Mank. Back to the Future star and Parkinson's disease activist Michael J Fox chats about his memoir No Time Like the Future: An Optimist Considers Mortality, Nina Sosanya talks about her new Christmas drama, TV chef Jamie Oliver explains how his new book 7 Ways manages to breathe new life into staple ingredients and comedian Tom Allen talks about his memoir No Shame. Plus, Dolly Parton makes a special appearance performing the seaonal staple I Saw Mommy Kissing Santa Claus

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Paralympic great Hannah Cockcroft, Great Britain sprinter Harry Aikines-Aryeetey, Olympic hockey champion Kate Richardson-Walsh and Toronto Wolfpack forward Jon Wilkin join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker for the quiz

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Rachel Burden present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by special guest Tim Peake. Plus, archive clips from Rick Stein, Keith Floyd and Nadiya Hussain

programme.title: Nigella's Cook, Eat, Repeat
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson shares recipes featuring one of her favourite ingredients - the anchovy. She prepares a creamy dressing for the fish, served with bitter leaves and walnuts, and then creates an elixir, combining silvery salted anchovies with garlic and oil. This not only makes an excellent dip for red chicory, but also the perfect accompaniment to a platter of sliced seared steak. Nigella creates an anchovy pasta dish of spaghetti with chard and chilli, and also makes a lemon blossom cocktail and a lemon and elderflower pudding

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's football, including Chelsea v Leeds United and Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League

programme.title: Live Snooker: UK Championship
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents coverage of the opening semi-final from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, played over the best of 11 frames. After facing Liang Wenbo in the quarter-final, China's Ding Junhui played another of his compatriots at this stage last year, defeating Yan Bingtao to book his place in the final against Scot Stephen Maguire. With commentary and analysis from Steve Davis, Alan McManus, John Parrott, Dennis Taylor, Ken Doherty, John Virgo and Stephen Hendry

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in a special 1990s edition of the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. The contestants are Tamara Beckwith, Saffron Sprackling, Clare Buckfield, John Pickard, Mr Motivator, Diane Youdale, Nick Moran and Vas Blackwood

programme.title: The Hit List
programme.desc: Marvin and Rochelle Humes present the music-based quiz show, in which contestants must name as many hit songs and artists as possible under intense pressure, with a £10,000 jackpot up for grabs for the winners. Across four rounds, their knowledge is put to the test, as they attempt to identify huge hits across various decades. In this episode, the contestants are mother and daughter Ali and Nevada from London, engaged couple Sean and Alan from Chester, and military drummer Ronnie and his partner Wesley from Aldershot

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: It's Musicals Week on Strictly, meaning that remaining contestants Bill, HRVY, Jamie, JJ, Maisie and Ranvir - minus Clara, who was voted off last week - are keen to dazzle as perform routines inspired by hit shows including Little Shop of Horrors, Phantom of the Opera, Chitty Chitty Bang Bang and A Chorus Line. However, it's also the quarter-final and with the final looming ever closer, the pressure is on. The extravaganza gets under way with a Priscilla, Queen of the Desert routine from the professionals, which features Gorka, Giovanni and Johannes dazzling in high heels and ever higher wigs. Presented Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's The Wheel
programme.desc: The award-winning comedian Michael McIntyre hosts a new Saturday-night gameshow, as contestants take on a colossal spinning wheel and answer questions in the hope of securing the a cash prize. Each episode, contestants are guided by a host of celebrity experts, who are on hand to help them find their way to a fortune

programme.title: Blackadder
programme.desc: Edmund receives a visit from the bloodthirsty Bishop of Bath and Wells, who threatens to do him a mischief with a red-hot poker over the matter of an unpaid loan. As he attempts to raise the £1,000 he needs, he finds himself being constantly summoned to the court by the Queen. Comedy, starring Rowan Atkinson and Miranda Richardson

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Dan Walker is joined by former players Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game - Baltimore Ravens v Dallas Cowboys at M&T Bank Stadium

programme.title: Keeping Rosy (2014)
programme.desc: A businesswoman who has devoted her life to running a media agency is betrayed by her partners. Emotional and angry, she commits an uncharacteristically violent act, and takes increasingly desperate measures to make amends and protect an innocent child from further harm. Thriller, starring Maxine Peake and Blake Harrison

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Roger Johnson and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures, featuring Chelsea v Leeds United at Stamford Bridge, Manchester City v Fulham at Etihad Stadium, West Ham United v Manchester United at London Stadium and Burnley v Everton at Turf Moor

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Sunday Politics Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Mark Carruthers looks at the political developments of the week and questions policy-makers on the key issues

programme.title: A Very Country Christmas
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Angellica Bell celebrate Christmas traditions from three distinct parts of Britain. They begin by joining Advent preparations in the Highlands, meeting a Christmas tree farmer, a candlemaker and a woman who makes a traditional Scottish delicacy

programme.title: Sunday Worship
programme.desc: The Rev Alan Miller leads a service for the Second Sunday of Advent from the Church of the Holy Rude in Stirling. With hymns from the Songs of Praise archives from across Scotland

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts David Harper and Charles Hanson help two teams look for items at the Oswestry Showground in Shropshire, while Natasha Raskin Sharp finds out about rare Faberge flowers

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley hosts the final of the BBC Young Chorister of the Year competition as six girls and boys perform in front of judges John Rutter, Laura Wright and Carl Jackson. Including a performance of O Holy Night by Aled Jones, Katherine Jenkins and all the finalists

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson helps a couple up sticks from Birmingham and spend £500,000 on a country home that allows them to lead the good life while being close enough to Hereford for regular visits to their daughter.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look back at some of the best-loved festive movies

programme.title: Nativity 2: Danger in the Manger! (2012)
programme.desc: A primary school teacher enters his class in a Song for Christmas competition, and ends up having to lead the busload of excitable kids - as well as his irresponsible teaching assistant - on a disastrous trip to Wales for the event. The journey also leads to an unwanted confrontation with his high-flying twin brother. Comedy sequel, starring David Tennant, Marc Wootton, Joanna Page and Jessica Hynes

programme.title: Blue Planet II
programme.desc: Wildlife that live thousands of kilometres from land, featuring footage of baby turtles using debris as life rafts and whales sharks embarking on lengthy voyages across the Pacific to the point where it is safest to give birth. Special pressure-proof cameras are used to film sperm whales hunting for squid a kilometre beneath the surface and the impact of plastic pollution on the oceans is revealed

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Sean Fletcher visit the Black Mountains, which straddle the border between Wales and England, to find out more about a drive to preserve ancient rural skills and traditions. Sean gets a bird's eye view of the spectacular landscape, while former thatcher Joe Sugg battles for supremacy in a hedge-laying competition. Joe Crowley investigates the challenges facing the Scottish salmon industry, and Adam Henson has high hopes for his new ram

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman present as the two lowest-scoring couples face each other in the dance-off - with a place in next week's semi-final up for grabs. Musical theatre star Marisha Wallace performs Climb Ev'ry Mountain

programme.title: His Dark Materials
programme.desc: Will and Lyra plan to steal the alethiometer back from Boreal by cutting straight into his house from Cittagazze using the knife, while Mrs Coulter meets an intrigued Mary, who is later given a mysterious mission by the Cave. At the Magisterium, MacPhail condemns Graves to imprisonment

programme.title: Small Axe
programme.desc: Fact-based drama following the life of award-winning writer from a young boy through to his early adult years. Having spent his childhood in a mostly white institutional care home with no love or family, Alex finally finds not only a sense of community for the first time in Brixton, but his identity and an ability to nurture his passion for music and DJing. When he is thrown in prison during the Brixton riots of 1981, he confronts his past and sees a path to healing. Starring Sheyi Cole, with Robbie Gee and Jonathan Jules, and written by Alastair Siddons and Steve McQueen, who also directed

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, featuring Tottenham Hotspur v Arsenal at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, Liverpool v Wolverhampton Wanderers at Anfield, Sheffield United v Leicester City at Bramall Lane and West Bromwich Albion v Crystal Palace at The Hawthorns

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Everton v Manchester City, Arsenal v Birmingham City and Chelsea v West Ham United

programme.title: StreetDance 2 (2012)
programme.desc: A dancer tries to put a humiliating defeat in a contest behind him by assembling an international dance crew to compete in a high-profile European event. He finds a perfect recruit in a Cuban salsa teacher, but his plan to combine their dance forms into a new style is threatened by their feelings for each other. Drama, starring Falk Hentschel, Tom Conti and Sofia Boutella

programme.title: The Vicar of Dibley
programme.desc: The Rev Geraldine Granger delivers online sermons for her parishioners during the pandemic, beginning by explaining the difference between metres and miles when it comes to social distancing. Richard Curtis's comedy returns for three mini-episodes, starring Dawn French

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon hosts a festive edition of the comedy panel show from 2016, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by Richard Osman, Tom Courtenay, Sara Pascoe and Chris Kamara. As usual, all participants will be hoping to deceive one another with plausible lies, such as Richard stating he has never seen Breaking Bad because of his Homes Under the Hammer addiction, Sir Tom claiming he enjoys whiskey on his Christmas morning cornflakes, and Chris explaining that his full name is actually Christmas Kamara. The team also presents a somewhat unusual take on the Nativity

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: Christmas is supposed to be a time for family and friends, so when it seems that this year Mrs Brown cannot please anyone, she wonders if it would have been better had she never been born. Enter Clyde, a mysterious figure claiming to be an angel, who tells the doting mother he can show her what life would have been like without her - and before she knows it, she finds herself in a world she doesn't recognise, and that doesn't recognise her. Brendan O'Carroll stars in this 2019 spoof of classic Christmas movie It's a Wonderful Life, with former Corrie star Kevin Kennedy (Curly Watts)

programme.title: The Man Who Wanted to Fly (2018)
programme.desc: The story of octogenarian farmer Bobby Coote's efforts to fulfil a lifelong ambition to fly a plane, following him as he cuts out a runway in his neighbour Sean's fields and even builds a hangar in this small rural community in Cavan. Bobby gets no encouragement from his brother Ernie, who thinks the whole thing is daft, but he is determined to take to the skies if it's the last thing he does

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: Imran Khan won power in Pakistan two years ago with a promise to root out corruption and take on the country's vested interests. So how's it going? Rising food prices and the Covid pandemic have left many Pakistanis feeling worse off, while the anti-corruption drive has become a political battleground. Stephen Sackur speaks to Ishaq Dar who was Pakistan's Finance Minister and a key lieutenant of former Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif. Now both men are trying to rally opposition to Imran Khan, but how much credibility do they have?

programme.desc: Live coverage of the joint session of the Science and Technology select committee and the Health and Social Care select committee on the coronavirus with evidence from Professor Dame Sally Davies, Chief Medical Officer for England (2010-2019), Sir Mark Walport, Government Chief Scientific Adviser (2013-2017), Sir Oliver Letwin, Cabinet Office Minister (2010-2016) and Lord Mark Sedwill, Cabinet Secretary (2018-2020).

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: The Home Affairs select committee session on Channel crossings, migrants, and asylum-seeking routes through the EU with evidence from the minister for Immigration Compliance and the Courts, Chris Philp, from Wednesday 2 December.

programme.title: Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal
programme.desc: Tom Brook marks the fortieth anniversary of former Beatle John Lennon's murder in New York on 8 December 1980. His untimely death was a slaying that shocked millions of fans around the world. In Lennon Remembered: A Reporter's Journal, the BBC's Tom Brook, the first British journalist to report live from the site of the New York shooting, takes stock of Lennon's life and the legacy of his music. Brook recounts what happened on the night Lennon died when he returned to his home, the Dakota apartment building on Manhattan's Upper West Side, with his wife Yoko Ono, and he hears from some of Lennon's closest confidantes as well as younger Lennon fans in America today. Drawing on a wealth of archive material, the programme includes Tom Brook's interview with the former Beatle's widow Yoko Ono, one of the first she gave, in which she discussed Lennon's life and death. The programme assesses John Lennon's powerful legacy 40 years on and asks whether his messages of love and peace are still re

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: Over 30 years on, the scale and magnitude of the Piper Alpha disaster continues to shock. Iona Ballantyne's father Bob was a well-known spokesman for survivors and their families, and a delayed response to grief was felt strongly by Iona years after his death. To understand and process her own thoughts and loss she visits other families, who like her, were direct witnesses to the consequences of the disaster and the changes it forced upon them

programme.title: Julie Fowlis: Ceol Aig Baile
programme.desc: Featuring multiple Grammy-winning music star Rosanne Cash, Dreamer's Circus from Denmark, May Erlewine from Michigan and virtuoso jazz pianist Kit Downes

programme.title: Gàidhlig Obar Dheathain (Aberdeen, Gaelic and the Gaels)
programme.desc: Twenty-five years since the Aberdeen Gaelic School opened, many in the city are against the language. Through the eyes of the Gaels who live there this film looks at the history of Gaelic in the area and why it is different from the other major cities in Scotland. Narrated by Patricia Macleod

programme.title: FUNC
programme.desc: Trump goes on holiday in the homeland, the Childrens' TV Presenters are falling out and the Vikings do not like their Pictish naked neighbours

programme.title: Alba Today
programme.desc: BBC Alba joins Radio nan Gàidheal, the BBC's Gaelic language radio service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz falls into the sewers, only to bump into Magoo, who thinks he's exploring the jungle. The latter mistakes Fizz for a lost child.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: In the desert, Fizz and Weasel plan a fireworks display for Fizz. Mountain Rescuer Magoo mistakes Weasel for a skier in distress.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: In the forest, Magoo sets up camp by the rare fruit patch, giving a warm welcome to a bear that he has mistaken for a wayward hiker.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: A mysterious tunnelling vehicle leaves a worker trapped in its wake and it is up to Gordon and Virgil to free him, while Parker and Penelope track down the digger

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny takes on one of the UK's toughest obstacle courses, but can he beat the clock and avoid the forfeit?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Craig of the Creek follows 10-year-old Craig Williams and his two friends, Kelsey and John Paul, as they go on adventures in a world of untamed, kid-dominated wilderness in the creek which acts as a utopia where children reign

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: The Mystery Inc. team is back and just as courageous, adventurous, humorous, and obsessed with cases as ever. When Scooby and Shaggy compete in a fast food eating competition, they meet their ultimate challenge against comedian Jim Gaffigan while contending with the Spectral Speedster, who is trying to stop the contest from finishing.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin feels the other Titans are spending too much time together so he encourages them to spend some time alone

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by singer Sophie Ellis-Bextor, while chefs Ravneet Gill and James Tanner provide their own recipes. Plus, James provides a masterclass in lentils and embarks on a food adventure at historic Goodwood House

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare some Christmas dishes, including ham in parsley sauce, and curried puffs and potato bombas. Lisa also prepares her grandmother's recipe for traditional mincemeat, which can be used in both mince pies and a festive frangipane layer cake

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James joins up with fellow chef Lisa Allen to explore Whitby, in James' home county of North Yorkshire. James pops into his favourite fish and chip shop before cooking tikka mackerel buns with fennel and apple salad for the crowds at Whitby bandstand. Lisa, meanwhile, pays a visit to Michelin-starred restaurant The Black Swan, to see how its huge kitchen garden provides almost all of the produce used in the kitchen

programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly travels to the 21st century, where he has to save his children from a terrible fate. However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: The final round of the obstacle-course challenge arrives, as Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara invite the most successful participants from throughout this series to return to the course and put their strength, speed and agility to the test once again

programme.title: Celebrity Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a star-studded edition of the game show, with teams consisting of Emmerdale stars Charley Webb and Matthew Wolfenden, The Chase's Jenny Ryan and singer Megan McKenna, and DJs Melvin Odoom and Rickie Haywood-Williams racing down the aisles to win a £10,000 jackpot for the charity of their choice

programme.title: Catchphrase: Catchiest Moments
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern presents a third compilation of some of his favourite moments from the quiz show, from the funniest answers to the strangest. Featuring previously unseen footage, and giving viewers a chance to play along at home as contestants make unexpected attempts to Say What They See

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents a special Emmerdale edition, as Mark Charnock, Charley Webb, Matthew Wolfenden and Amy Walsh pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: The Jonathan Ross Show
programme.desc: The host is joined on tonight's show by guests Michael Palin and Kylie Minogue, as well as his co-stars on the next series of The Masked Singer, Rita Ora, Davina McCall and Mo Gilligan

programme.title: Through the Keyhole
programme.desc: Keith Lemon hosts a revival of the classic game show. Today's panellists are Dani Dyer, Martin Kemp and Paddy McGuinness, who are given a tour of three mystery celebrity homes, and are challenged to guess the identities of the famous occupants

programme.title: The Keith & Paddy Picture Show
programme.desc: Keith Lemon teams up with Paddy McGuinness to remake Steven Spielberg's 1993 dinosaur-based classic Jurassic Park. The duo are helped by Sian Gibson, Sally Phillips, George Potts, Shaun Ryder and Bernie Clifton to reimagine the story of genetically engineered dinosaurs that escape from an eccentric tycoon's safari park and go on the rampage

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Having made it through to the quarter-finals, two families go head to head for a place in the semi-finals of the tournament, aiming to move a step closer to winning the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Family and a holiday of a lifetime. Hosted by Anne Hegerty

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's a beach lifeguard but is actually on an ice floe and the swimmers are penguins.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel are after the president's jokebook. But Magoo, thinking he's in his dojo, enters the high-security unit where it's kept...

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo's going to miss his flight. Unfortunately, he's not at the airport, but the supermarket, where Fizz and Weasel are hatching their latest plan.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The team is dispatched to assist when a dormant volcano suddenly erupts, validating the outlandish warnings an eccentric scientist had been making for years

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's dare is to drive a tank blindfolded. Can he get across the course and knock down the cardboard box wall?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood film. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Madcap slapstick and mayhem as the celebrated cat and mouse plot against each other

programme.title: DC Superhero Girls
programme.desc: Leslie Willis, the meanest girl in school, becomes a social media sensation by posting mean-spirited videos.

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Robin teaches the other Titans about the difference between the Imperial System and the Metric System

programme.title: Best Walks with a View with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: An exploration of scenic Britain on foot, beginning in north Wales where she heads out beside the Menai Straits overlooking Snowdonia. During the trek, Julia seeks out the lost prototype of the first ever Land Rover, which locals believe may be buried in the Anglesey sand-dunes where its inventor first put it through trials almost 70 years ago. The expert walker also discovers why Prince William and his wife have a soft spot for the romantic St Llanddwyn Island, home to the Welsh St Valentine. Finally, she meets mussel and oyster pickers, getting to taste their wares in a sunset beach picnic

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh welcomes actors Sheila Hancock and Jane Horrocks to the farm and celebrates the best Christmas trees and puddings

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Alex Beresford and Ria Hebden visit the country's most fascinating attractions, sharing extraordinary stories of human endeavour and must-see places

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A series of killings in Midsomer St Claire seem to be inspired by macabre images on a medieval fresco recently discovered in the church crypt. As the village prepares for storms and flooding, it appears that someone is using ancient torture methods to punish modern-day 'sinners'. With help from sidekick Charlie Nelson, Barnaby tries to get to the bottom of the case, and unearths a long-held secret that takes him by surprise. With Rebecca Front, Andrea Lowe, Vincent Regan and Roy Hudd

programme.title: Apollo 13 (1995)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama about the ill-fated 1970 Apollo 13 mission to the moon. Crew leader Jim Lovell is faced with a nightmare when the mission suffers an on-board explosion - leaving the team stranded in space with a dwindling oxygen supply while flight controllers and engineers in Houston try to find a way to bring the crew safely back to Earth. With Tom Hanks, Kevin Bacon, Bill Paxton, Ed Harris and Gary Sinise

programme.title: 5 Gold Rings
programme.desc: Mother and daughter duo Kay and Paula take on best friends Ryan and Rob in a bid to win more than £50k. All they have to do is find the answer on the giant interactive floor and put a ring on it. Phillip Schofield hosts

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Alan Johnson, Katherine Ryan and Jimmy Bullard answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity. Hosted by Ben Shephard

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire: The Million Pound Question
programme.desc: A look back at Robert Brydges' lucrative appearance in 2001, and emotions run high with the appearance of Rachel Golding from the first series. Plus, Chris Evans' memorable attempts to boost his chosen charities' good fortune, and celebrities such as John Barnes, Dominic Brunt and host Jeremy Clarkson recall some of their fond memories from the quiz show

programme.title: Gary Barlow's Night at the Museum
programme.desc: The award-winning singer/songwriter performs tracks from his new album, Music Played By Humans, as well as some of his greatest hits. Gary is accompanied by an orchestra at London's Natural History Museum, and will be joined by a range of special guests, including Michael Buble, Jack Whitehall, Beverley Knight, Alfie Boe, Rick Astley, Jason Donovan, Alesha Dixon, Ronan Keating and Sebastian Yatra

programme.title: The Road to Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jane Horrocks and Jessie Wallace star in a one-off drama telling the story of how the long-running soap was born. Tony Warren joins producer Harry Elton and director Derek Bennett as they struggle to create the programme, faced with opposition from ITV's formidable bosses Sidney and Cecil Bernstein, while Margaret Morris begins the hunt for the show's cast. With David Dawson, Stephen Berkoff, Celia Imrie and Lynda Baron

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents action from Thruxton, featuring round 10 of both the Porsche Carrera Cup GB and Ginetta GT4 Championship

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip. Presented by Lorraine Kelly

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Celebrity interviews and studio discussion from a female perspective

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Wolverhampton, where a 300-year-old book turns up on Simon Schneider's table and Henry Nicholls has his eye on a diamond ring

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Omid Djalili hosts the quiz, with contestants Romy, Carla, Emily, Francesca, Neil, Jonathan, Calum, Chris and Sally each assigned a number between one and nine, with the four that make the final trying to win the jackpot defined by those numbers. Each player's number is also the total of questions they must answer correctly in the final game against the clock

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Lucy, Paddy and Karen pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rishi has an accident, Meena has a plan, and Paul is uneasy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen's fate hangs in the balance as the trial draws to a close, the residents band together to halt Ray's bulldozer, and Daniel uncovers the truth about Adam and Carla

programme.title: Coronation Street: 60 Unforgettable Years
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley narrates a celebration of the diamond anniversary of the programme, exploring the legacy of the world's longest-running TV soap opera. There are revelations from the show's stars past and present, including audition nerves, fallings out, memorable location shoots, as well as their memories of the storylines that gripped the nation

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Hotel concierge Nigel is house hunting for love with the help of his best friend Rufus. The singleton is looking to find someone special and give them the five-star treatment. To do so, Nigel visits three London-based women's homes and is faced with a tricky choice when it comes to picking one to take on a date. There is a gin-loving interior stylist, an Ibiza party girl and a former ballet dancer. Who will be heading back to his?

programme.title: Good Morning Britain

programme.title: Dickinson's Real Deal
programme.desc: David Dickinson and the team are in Wolverhampton, where a 300-year-old book turns up on Simon Schneider's table and Henry Nicholls has his eye on a diamond ring

programme.title: Winning Combination
programme.desc: Omid Djalili hosts the quiz, with contestants Romy, Carla, Emily, Francesca, Neil, Jonathan, Calum, Chris and Sally each assigned a number between one and nine, with the four that make the final trying to win the jackpot defined by those numbers. Each player's number is also the total of questions they must answer correctly in the final game against the clock

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Chris, Lucy, Paddy and Karen pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rishi has an accident, Meena has a plan, and Paul is uneasy

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen's fate hangs in the balance as the trial draws to a close, the residents band together to halt Ray's bulldozer, and Daniel uncovers the truth about Adam and Carla

programme.title: Coronation Street: 60 Unforgettable Years
programme.desc: Joanna Lumley narrates a celebration of the diamond anniversary of the programme, exploring the legacy of the world's longest-running TV soap opera. There are revelations from the show's stars past and present, including audition nerves, fallings out, memorable location shoots, as well as their memories of the storylines that gripped the nation

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: It'll Be Alright on the Night
programme.desc: David Walliams presents a compilation of TV clangers from the past year's programmes. Featuring live on-air blunders from Stormzy, Robbie Williams, Craig Revel Horwood, Jonathan Ross, and Ant & Dec, as well as a series of outtakes starring Will.i.am and Jeremy Clarkson. Plus, two toddlers run rings around Kay Burley, and Vicky Pattison forgets the name of the show she's presenting

programme.title: All Elite Wrestling: Dynamite
programme.desc: Hard-hitting action from the world of All Elite Wrestling, featuring all of the biggest stars on the roster, including Adam Page, Chris Jericho, Cody and Jon Moxley

programme.title: Back to Mine
programme.desc: Hotel concierge Nigel is house hunting for love with the help of his best friend Rufus. The singleton is looking to find someone special and give them the five-star treatment. To do so, Nigel visits three London-based women's homes and is faced with a tricky choice when it comes to picking one to take on a date. There is a gin-loving interior stylist, an Ibiza party girl and a former ballet dancer. Who will be heading back to his?

programme.title: London Zoo: An Extraordinary Year
programme.desc: In March, ZSL London Zoo and its sister zoo Whipsnade fell silent as potential visitors stayed away. This two-part documentary reveals how a dedicated skeleton staff continued to care for the 20,000 animals in Regent's Park and the Dunstable Downs in Bedfordshire. Gemma and Meg must find a way to ensure that the pregnancy care for an endangered okapi isn't thrown off track by the lack of staff. Jamie continues the arachnid breeding programme, introducing a couple of huntsman spiders to each other - but it's a fraught business that could end in love-making or cannibalism

programme.title: Kate: The Making of a Modern Queen
programme.desc: Profile of Kate Middleton, how she has changed as person since her marriage to Prince William and what she means for the royal family. From comparatively modest circumstances, the Middleton family could not be more different from the Windsors, yet this is exactly what Prince William wanted - a royal life combined with comfortable marital stability and a continuation of the easier relationship with the British people started by his mother, Princess Diana. The couple met at St Andrew's University in 2001 and after several ups and downs were married at Westminster Abbey in 2011. Contributors include Anthony Seldon, Ferdinand Mount, Catherine Mayer, Emily Nash, Katie Nichol and Sarah Bradford

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The inspector and his sidekick DS Hathaway investigate the murder of a talented maths student. The prime suspect is the victim's ex-boyfriend, who is convinced his uncle has set him up, but old case papers belonging to Morse reveal the boy's family is hiding a dark secret. Laurence Fox and Rebecca Front co-star alongside Kevin Whately

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective suspects a connection between the death of a student with secret plans for advancement and the killing of well-known criminal Nicky Turnball, who was in Oxford to speak at the Union. As Lewis and Hathaway eliminate suspects from a long list of the crook's enemies, the investigation brings him into contact with old flame Diane - who also happens to be Turnball's wife. Kevin Whately stars, with Laurence Fox and Gina McKee

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: When a housewife is found hanged in her home, the police initially mark it down as suicide. However, a meeting with an Oxford law professor prompts a sceptical Lewis and Hathaway to believe something more sinister is afoot - a suspicion confirmed by the death of another woman. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective investigates a double poisoning, aiming to prove the innocence of Elinor Carlisle, the chief suspect in the deaths of her aunt and her rival in love. However, it emerges that she is the only person with a motive, opportunity and means to administer the poison. Crime drama, starring David Suchet, Elisabeth Dermot-Walsh, Diana Quick, Paul McGann and Rupert Penry-Jones

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When the detective is called to investigate what appears to be a freak accident at Lady Matilda's College, he uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar incidents across Oxford. Despite Thursday's scepticism, he becomes convinced the accidents are the result of foul play. Lady Matilda's is in the middle of a referendum campaign to decide whether or not the college should become co-educational - and when a woman is attacked while walking alone on a towpath, Endeavour and Thursday try to find the assailant before they strike again. Drama, starring Shaun Evans

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is brought in to investigate when a former Met police officer is stabbed to death outside a nightclub in Newcastle. The team initially suspects a crime of passion after it emerges the victim had recently rekindled his affair with his childhood sweetheart, whose husband has a history of violence. However, Vera uncovers evidence pointing to a more complex case involving inside information and the hostile takeover of a brewery. What did the deceased find out that cost him his life? Guest starring Jill Halfpenny, Christine Bottomley, Ralph Ineson and Liam Cunningham

programme.title: Vanity Fair
programme.desc: Becky masters the fine art of living well on nothing a year, proving that a respectable public appearance is all you need to get credit. Her flirtation with Lord Steyne at least provides a reliable source of income, but the Sedley family are less fortunate. With her finances in tatters, a devastated Amelia is forced to send her son to live with the Osbournes. Becky rises to the very top of high society, even securing a royal audience - but Steyne expects something in return for his patronage, and when Rawdon is accosted by bailiffs, he seizes his chance

The elephant keepers at Whipsnade are desperate for the Asian elephants to mate, although it seems there are no such problems with the small-clawed otters when keeper Laura notices they have had their first babies

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the death of a maintenance engineer found shot in the head in the basement of the Bodleian Library. A search of the man's house reveals a stash of valuable volumes and a connection to the local Gamblers Anonymous group, and the detectives go on to expose a scam involving two Oxford academics. Drama, guest starring Neil Pearson and Haydn Gwynne, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The death of Oxford don RG Cole leads to the shady underworld of unlicensed boxing and the involvement of academic Richard Helm. Investigations reveal the dead man's student Jack Roth was involved in a complex love triangle, and when the young man's rival is found dead in an apparent suicide, Lewis suspects foul play and discovers an old connection to Helm - along with a link to his former mentor Morse. Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox star

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: Ruby encounters new challenges as a blistering heatwave hits Kerala, while Lydia decides she is ready for her first solo night shift. Return of the medical drama set in southern Indian, starring Amrita Acharia and Amanda Redman

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital

Meanwhile, Ruby and Gabriel treat a patient with a mystery condition - and Gabriel reveals something about his past. Drama, starring Amanda Redman, Amrita Acharia and James Krishna Floyd

programme.title: The Loch
programme.desc: A body is pulled from the loch and soon identified by the pathologist - while a photograph in the dead man's wallet provides Annie and Quigley with a vital clue. Annie tries to gather further evidence by talking to a desperate Mhari, who refuses to believe the terrifying possibility she is presented with concerning her own son. Albrighton is threatened with arrest by Quigley, but nevertheless continues to pursue his own theory about the killings - even if it means putting his life on the line to prove himself right

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A middle-aged Oxford graduate is found murdered, setting in motion an investigation that leads Lewis and Hathaway to one of the university's most senior dons, whose respectable exterior conceals a strange past. programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ashley visits Don in prison, and Brennan implicates him in the factory fire. Vera breaks down over Terry. Alma tells Mike she wants them to make a fresh start abroad

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oswald Cooper is the prime suspect in the rape of a teenage girl, but presents an alibi supported by three pillars of the community. When Cooper is murdered, Lewis discovers a web of deceit going back 20 years, in which the dead man was used by the trio to cover up a sordid series of secrets - but the case soon takes on a personal dimension for the inspector. Kwame Kwei-Armah, Sean McGinley, Richard McCabe and Daniela Nardini guest star, with Kevin Whately

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth finds himself in interesting company as English socialites and American millionaires rub shoulders on board a train bound for the French Riviera. Passengers include oil magnate Rufus Van Aldin (Elliott Gould) and his daughter Ruth (Jaime Murray) - to whom he has given a magnificent ruby known as the Heart of Fire. With members of the aristocracy crammed into a confined space with their husbands, lovers and a renowned gemstone, murder cannot be far away. With Lindsay Duncan, Roger Lloyd Pack and James D'Arcy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Florrie Lindley takes over the corner shop and Ken Barlow clashes with his father Frank. The first episode shown on December 9, 1960

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Val decides to give people something to talk about, and Betty is made senior barmaid at the Rovers in this episode from December 9, 1970

programme.title: The Trouble with Maggie Cole
programme.desc: Six-part drama following the lives of residents in the close-knit coastal resort of Thurlbury. Local historian Maggie Cole is flattered when she is interviewed for a radio show about village life, but she inadvertently shares too much detail about the locals and their private lives. Starring Dawn French and Julie Hesmondhalgh

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A serial killer begins targeting members of a religious group that claims to provide guidance for lost souls. As the investigation proceeds, it becomes clear that Hathaway, who once trained for the priesthood, knows more about the case than he is willing to reveal - causing Lewis to consider him as a suspect. Guest starring Ian McNeice and Rachael Stirling alongside Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox
